mappings.rationale: Provides the broad framework for understanding personality theories, measurement, adjustment, and mental disorders.
Directly covers major categories of mental disorders (section D.1) including schizophrenia, affective disorders, anxiety disorders, and their treatment.
Directly covers psychoanalytic theories of personality including id, ego, superego, life and death instincts (section A.2).
Freud developed foundational psychoanalytic personality theory including id, ego, superego, and defense mechanisms (sections A.2, C.3).
Jung's concept of the collective unconscious is specifically mentioned in psychoanalytic theories of personality (section A.2).
Directly relevant to treatment approaches for mental disorders and personality disturbances (sections D.3, D.4).
A major therapeutic approach for treating mental disorders including anxiety disorders, depression, and obsessive-compulsive disorders (section D.1).
Directly covers assessment and treatment of personality disturbances and mental disorders, including measurement of personality (sections B, D).
Schizophrenia is specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (section D.1).
Affective disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (section D.1), and depression is the most prevalent.
Anxiety disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (section D.1).
Covers the history and cultural understanding of mental disorders, relevant to prevalence and epidemiology of mental disorders (section D.2).
Autism is a disorder of infancy or childhood, a category specifically named among major mental disorders (section D.1).
Personality disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (section D.1).
Substance use disorders are relevant to personality disturbances and mental disorders, involving defense mechanisms and coping responses.
Organic mental disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (section D.1).
Directly covers the subjective aspect of personality and development of awareness of self (section C.1).
Relevant to affective disorders (section D.1) and the emotional dimensions of personality functioning and adjustment.
Relevant to social analytic theories of personality (section A.3) and the social stresses that challenge personality functioning (section C.2).
Relevant to dissociative disorders (section D.1) and the subjective dimension of personality and self-awareness (section C.1).
Covers psychological assessment in legal contexts, relevant to personality measurement (section B) and personality disorders (section D.1).
Covers the neurological basis of personality, relevant to physiological theories of personality (section A.1) and organic mental disorders.
Covers the neural basis of personality functioning, relevant to physiological theories and the neuroscience of mental disorders.
Psychosexual disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (section D.1).
Relevant to personality functioning and adjustment (section C), and the psychological dimensions of well-being and affective states.
Directly relevant to the prevalence of mental disorders and epidemiology (section D.2).
Relevant to the subjective aspect of personality and development of self-awareness, and to social analytic theories of personality (sections A.3, C.1).
Relevant to social analytic theories of personality (section A.3) and social stresses on personality functioning (section C.2).
